channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Astronauts on International Space Station told to shelter as repairs under way to fix air leaks - BBC
item.description:
1. Astronauts on International Space Station told to shelter as repairs under way to fix air leaks  BBC
  2. NASA directs its ISS crew members to board spacecraft amid leak repair attempt  CNN
  3. Nasa tells ISS astronauts to shelter during air leak repair attempt  BBC
  4. International Space Station astronauts resume normal duties after evacuation order  The Guardian
  5. NASA orders astronauts to take shelter after new leak aboard the International Space Station  AP News

item.title: NASA's dead Mars orbiter MAVEN will crash into the Red Planet in the next 100 years. It's not the only probe in the Mars morgue - Space
item.description:
1. NASA's dead Mars orbiter MAVEN will crash into the Red Planet in the next 100 years. It's not the only probe in the Mars morgue  Space
  2. NASA Says Goodbye to its Longtime Mars MAVEN Mission  The New York Times
  3. NASA's MAVEN Will Crash Into Mars Within A 100 Years – Joining One Of The Solar System's Largest Spacecraft Graveyards  IFLScience
  4. NASA Says Farewell to MAVEN Mars Mission, Hosts Media Call Today  NASA (.gov)
  5. Like 'the loss of a loved one': NASA's Mars orbiter MAVEN is officially dead after months of radio silence  Space

item.title: Upgraded ‘Severe’ Northern Lights Alert For 25 States Thursday - Yahoo
item.description:
1. Upgraded ‘Severe’ Northern Lights Alert For 25 States Thursday  Yahoo
  2. A powerful geomagnetic storm will give a chance to see northern lights in North Carolina  WXII
  3. UPDATE: Aurora forecast for Vt., NY, and NH, Thursday night due to ‘G3′ geomagnetic storm  WCAX
  4. Aurora alert! 4 Earth-bound CMEs could spark northern lights as far south as Illinois and Oregon tonight  Space
  5. Auroras could be visible in more states with forecast 'strong' geomagnetic storm  ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

item.title: Wind from Milky Way's supermassive black hole is finally discovered - Reuters
item.description:
1. Wind from Milky Way's supermassive black hole is finally discovered  Reuters
  2. Massive Void Near Milky Way’s Black Hole Could Solve a 50-Year Mystery  Gizmodo
  3. Even quiet black holes create winds, new Milky Way observations reveal  Science News
  4. Astronomers just solved a 50-year-old mystery about the Milky Way’s black hole  Scientific American
  5. Milky Way black hole's missing wind finally found after a half-century-long search  Phys.org

item.title: Queen bees emerge from special wax chambers - Chemical & Engineering News
item.description:
1. Queen bees emerge from special wax chambers  Chemical & Engineering News
  2. Queen cell architecture shapes honey bee queen development  Nature

  4. “That innocent question hit me like a lightning bolt." Scientist's two-year-old son inspires discovery of new type of worker honeybee  BBC Wildlife Magazine
  5. A secret to making a queen bee may lie in the wax around it  Science News

item.title: Giant fan-shaped structure found under East Antarctica - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Giant fan-shaped structure found under East Antarctica  Phys.org
  2. Scientists discover vast hidden structure beneath Antarctica’s ice  ScienceDaily
  3. A fan-shaped subglacial basin province in East Antarctica formed by rotational extension  Nature
  4. An Australian Navy submarine detects unknown structures beneath Antarctica’s Dotson Ice Shelf, and the find suggests the seafloor there is more active than it looks  ECOticias.com
  5. A Swedish research robot submarine detects unknown structures beneath Antarctica’s Dotson Ice Shelf, and the find suggests ice shelf’s underside is more complex than expected  The Times of India

item.title: New Velociraptor cousin was a '4-winged' dragon that hunted prey from the trees of ancient China, fossil find hints - Live Science
item.description:
1. New Velociraptor cousin was a '4-winged' dragon that hunted prey from the trees of ancient China, fossil find hints  Live Science
  2. Rare fossil belonged to a gliding creature that hunted birds  CNN
  3. New species of dinosaur, a cousin of Velociraptor, probably glided on four “wings” and hunted early birds  Field Museum
  4. New Microraptorine Dinosaur Discovered in China  Sci.News
  5. Carnegie museum paleontologist co-leads discovery of a new dinosaur species in China  Pittsburgh Post-Gazette

item.title: Bumblebees can spontaneously solve problems, study finds - Ars Technica
item.description:

  4. ‘They surprise me every time’: bees can use tools to solve problems, study finds  The Guardian
  5. Bumblebees use tools to solve complex problems—despite not being trained to do so  Scientific American

item.title: On a Saturday afternoon in May 2026, a rock about three feet across hit the atmosphere over New England at 75,000 mph and broke apart with the energy of roughly 300 tons of TNT, and the boom carried from Delaware to Montreal, farther than any fragment e - Space Daily
item.description:
1. On a Saturday afternoon in May 2026, a rock about three feet across hit the atmosphere over New England at 75,000 mph and broke apart with the energy of roughly 300 tons of TNT, and the boom carried from Delaware to Montreal, farther than any fragment e  Space Daily
  2. Meteor explodes off coast of Massachusetts, causing loud boom  CBS News
  3. Meteor that landed in Cape Cod Bay may have been made of iron and was unusually dense, NASA says  The Boston Globe
  4. Meteors are 'extremely common.' What makes the one over New England 'rare'?  Phys.org
  5. Massive boom over northeastern US was a meteor explosion as powerful as 300 tons of TNT, NASA confirms  Space

item.title: Scientists say the gold in wedding rings, teeth, and family heirlooms was forged in cosmic catastrophes long before our sun existed - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Scientists say the gold in wedding rings, teeth, and family heirlooms was forged in cosmic catastrophes long before our sun existed  Space Daily
  2. On Physics: The golden age of neutron stars  Aspen Daily News
  3. Almost all the gold on Earth — every wedding ring, every coin, every gram in every bank vault — was forged in the collision of dead stars billions of years ago, in events so violent that a single one can produce hundreds of Earth-masses of gold, and the heavy  Space Daily

item.description:

  4. Scientists decoded the genome of Earth's longest-living shark and found a multilayered genetic blueprint for surviving centuries  ecoportal.net
  5. First whole-genome sequence of a Greenland shark holds clues to their extreme longevity  Live Science
